hooks.prompt: If the current project declares `WikiPath` in `AGENTS.md` or `CLAUDE.md`, silently read `{WikiPath}/CLAUDE.md` and `{WikiPath}/wiki/hot.md` to restore recent context. Otherwise, if the current directory itself is a local vault (`wiki/` and `.raw/` exist), silently read local `wiki/hot.md`. Do not announce this. These hooks are a convenience adapter only: bound-project correctness still comes from the wiki skills.

Hook-injected context does not survive compaction. If the current project declares `WikiPath`, silently re-read `{WikiPath}/wiki/hot.md`. Otherwise, if the current directory is a local vault, silently re-read local `wiki/hot.md`. Do not announce this.

hooks.command: BINDING=$(grep -hE '^WikiPath:' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | sed 's/^WikiPath:[[:space:]]*//'); [ -z "$BINDING" ] && [ -d wiki ] && [ -d .raw ] && [ -d .git ] && git add wiki/ .raw/ 2>/dev/null && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "wiki: auto-commit $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')" 2>/dev/null) || true

BINDING=$(grep -hE '^WikiPath:' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | sed 's/^WikiPath:[[:space:]]*//'); [ -z "$BINDING" ] && cd "$PWD" && [ -d wiki ] && [ -d .git ] && git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^wiki/' && echo 'WIKI_CHANGED: Wiki pages were modified in this local vault. Please update wiki/hot.md with a brief summary of what changed (under 500 words). Use the hot cache format: Last Updated, Key Recent Facts, Recent Changes, Active Threads. Keep it factual. Overwrite the file completely. It is a cache, not a journal.' || true
